title: Algebra, Geometry & Applied Trigonometry
subject: Mathematics
description: Solve linear equations, quadratic formulas, Pythagorean theorem, right-triangle trigonometry, and slope-intercept graphing.
learningObjectives: Solve single-variable linear equations (ax + b = c) and systems of equations.
Apply Pythagorean Theorem (a^2 + b^2 = c^2) to find unknown distances.
Calculate trigonometric ratios (Sine, Cosine, Tangent) in practical applications.

lessons.title: Pythagorean Theorem & Structural Layouts
lessons.content: In any right-angled triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides: a^2 + b^2 = c^2. Builders use the 3-4-5 rule to ensure corners are perfectly square (3^2 + 4^2 = 9 + 16 = 25 = 5^2).

lessons.title: Linear Equations & Slope-Intercept Form
lessons.content: The slope-intercept equation is y = mx + b, where m is the slope (rise over run) and b is the y-intercept. Linear equations model constant rates of change, such as battery discharge rates over time.

flashcards.definition: Formula for right triangles: a^2 + b^2 = c^2.
The longest side of a right-angled triangle, opposite the right angle.
The steepness of a line calculated as Rise divided by Run (delta y / delta x).

questions.question: If a right triangle has legs of length a = 6 meters and b = 8 meters, what is the length of the hypotenuse c?
In the linear equation y = 3x + 5, what is the slope (m) of the line?

practicalChallenge.title: The 3-4-5 Square Corner Builder Test
practicalChallenge.instruction: Using a measuring tape and string, lay out a 3-foot by 4-foot right triangle on the floor. Verify that the diagonal distance equals exactly 5 feet!
